data.title: Client-Server Model
data.description: WhatsApp employs a client-server model to facilitate messaging services between users.
data.technical_spec: The architecture utilizes a centralized server that manages message routing, delivery, and storage.

data.title: End-to-End Encryption
data.description: Messages are encrypted on the sender's device and decrypted on the receiver's device to ensure privacy.
data.technical_spec: WhatsApp uses the Signal Protocol for encryption, which is known for its strong security features.

data.title: XMPP Protocol
data.description: WhatsApp originally used the Extensible Messaging and Presence Protocol (XMPP) for message transmission.
data.technical_spec: XMPP is an open-standard communications protocol primarily for instant messaging.

data.title: Erlang Backend
data.description: The server backend is built using Erlang, a language designed for scalable and highly available systems.
data.technical_spec: Erlang provides benefits like low latency, high throughput, and distributed computing capabilities.

data.title: Distributed Database Management
data.description: WhatsApp employs distributed databases to handle the large volumes of user and message data.
data.technical_spec: These databases are optimized for speed and reliability, crucial for real-time communications.
